evals.name: export-first-project-inventory
evals.prompt: I have access to a large ChatGPT project and want a defensible capture before we put anything in our repository. Give me the first-pass plan and the exact local record I should create.

expectations.text: Response names `project_capture_manifest.json` as the inventory record.
Response uses `conversation_id` rather than title as the primary match key.
Response separates an immutable archive outside the repository from `custom-gpts/ingestion/` staging.

evals.name: branch-complete-normalization
evals.prompt: Our export parser produces a clean single transcript by following current_node. What must change so we can claim to have preserved every turn and regenerated answer?

expectations.text: Response says to retain every key in the conversation `mapping` object.
Response explicitly rejects serializing only the `current_node` path.
Response requires `node_id`, `parent_id`, and `child_ids` in normalized nodes.
Response uses `source_locator` in the form `mapping.<node_id>` or identifies it as required.

evals.name: unmatched-thread-and-public-boundary
evals.prompt: A thread visible in the project UI has no export match, and the project may contain private material. Tell me what to do before we write a distilled repository document.

expectations.text: Response assigns `no_match` and opens an exception rather than dropping the thread.
Response identifies Browser-based capture as the recovery path.
Response includes the exception states `open`, `recovered`, or `accepted_loss`.
Response distinguishes raw evidence from a public-safe artifact lane.
